summary:
The patient had gunshot wounds suggesting they were within range when shot due to visible tattoos near the entrance site; confirmation came through finding bullet fragments inside their body pointing towards a nearby shooter position during the incident.
The evidence suggests she received multiple injuries including those caused by bullets discharged over relatively small distances given the noted physical characteristics like tattoo discoloration surrounding one injury along with identified remnants internally supporting closeness between assailant and target location. Agent
 To summarize:

- Visible tattooing/blackening around the entry wound indicated the gun was held closely or moderately far but still proximate enough to cause significant trauma.
- Presence of bullet fragments confirmed substantial penetration leading to deeper tissue involvement typical of medium-to-short ranged shootings based on standard firearm ballistics findings. \boxed{Bullet Fragments} Summary: 
Visible tattooing and blackening around the entry wound suggested moderate-closeness while bullet fragments verified deep penetration characteristic of mid-close range shooting. 

This summary captures both visual indications implying proximity and direct anatomical proof confirming the nature of impact.
